content_en:
20. To Mention Himself Only if He is the Imam It is reported that the Prophet (2), said:

Let no one be the Imam of a people, and then only mention

himself in the du‘ a, and leave them out. If he does so, then he

214 has cheated them.

Although this hadith might not be authentic, the meaning of it is applicable, as the Imam is responsible for the welfare of the entire congregation. Therefore, when he makes a dw’ 4, such as in the witr or qunut prayer, he should not say, “O Allah, forgive me, and increase my knowledge!” but rather, “O Allah! Forgive us, and increase our knowledge,” and so forth. This only applies to the du‘a that is done out loud. As for the dua that one does silently, such as in sujad (prostration) or at the end of the prayer, then the Imam is allowed to pray only for himself.

214 Reported by al-Tirmidhi (# 357), Aba Dawad (# 90), and Ahmad (5/ 250). Al-Tirmidhi said that it is hasan, but Ahmad Shakir pointed out that it might have some weakness in it (2/190). Al-Albani stated that it is weak

"THE RECOMMENDED TIMES FOR Du'4

Of the great mercy of Allah is that He has favoured certain times over others. Thus, He has distinguished these timings so that the worshipper can eagerly anticipate their arrival, and thus pray earnestly and sincerely. Had all timings been the same, the worshipper would not have had the enthusiasm and fervor that exists during more blessed times.

Therefore, it is important that the one who desires that his dua be answered utilise these times, and ensure that his dua be more frequent and sincere during them.

1. Du‘a@ in the Last Third of the Night

During this time, when most of creation is in a deep sleep, the true worshipper is awake, earnestly praying to his Creator, reflecting upon Allah’s creation, the heavens and earth. In fact, Allah describes the true believers as those who:

Zeon ret pee @ Opts oh sty) “and, in the hours of dawn, they seek forgiveness from their

Not only that, but Allah Himself comes down to His servant,

and opens for Him the doors of Mercy and Forgiveness. Aba Hurayrah narrated that the Prophet (#8) said:

2 seerah al-Dhariyat, 18.

“Our Lord descends every night, during the last third of it, to the skies of this world, and asks: ‘Who is making du’ a to Me, so that I can respond to him? Who is asking Me, so that I can give him? Who is asking for My forgiveness, so that I can forgive him?’”?"*

And ‘Amr ibn ‘Absah reported that the Prophet (#8) said:

“The closest any worshipper can be to his Lord is during the last part of the night, so if you can be amongst those who remember Allah at that time, then do so”

This is a huge incentive, then, for any person who truly desires

to come closer to Allah, all Glory and Praise be to Him, and wishes

.to have his sins forgiven, and his dua@ responded to, to wake up

when everyone else is asleep, and remember Allah privately, and all the while praying to Him.

Furthermore Aba Sa‘id and Aba Hurayrah both reported that the Prophet (#8) said:

“Allah waits until the first third of the night has passed, then He descends down to the skies of this world and says: ‘Is there any that seeks forgiveness? Is there any who wishes to repent? Is there any who is asking? Is there any who is making a du‘ a?’

And this continues, until the break of dawn”.

“Our Lord — all Glory and Praise be to Him — comes down in the last third of the night to the skies of this world, and He says: ‘Who is there that is making a dw’ @, so that I can respond to him? Whois there that is asking Me, so that I can give him? Who is there that is seeking my forgiveness, so that I can forgive him?”

216 Reported by al-Bukhari and Muslim, from Aba Hurayrah, as reported in Sahih al-Jami' # 8021.

Authentic, narrated by al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa’i, and al-Hakim from ‘Amr

ibn ‘Absah, and authenticated by al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami’ # 1173.

718 Authentic, narrated by Ahmad and Muslim from Aba Hurayrah and Aba Sa‘id al-Khudri.

219 Reported by al-Bukhari (#1145), Muslim (# 758) and others.

Hence why Abi Bakr al-Tartashi said, “And this chapter is closed by stating: He is not a wise man who has a need to Allah, yet he sleeps away his need at night!”

So if a person wishes that his du'@ be answered, this is the best time to ask.

2, An Hour of the Night

In addition to the hadith pertaining to du‘a at the last third of the night, there are also ahadith informing us that there is a general time at night during which du'as are responded to. The Prophet (88) said:

There is at night an hour, ne Muslim happens to be asking

Allah any matter of this world or the Hereafter, except that he will be given it, and this (occurs) every night.”!

3. dua When the Adhan is Called This is based on the hadith of the Prophet (#8):

“Two (du'as) are never rejected, or rarely rejected: the du‘a during the call for prayer, and the du‘a during the calamity,

when the two armies attack each other”.

“When the prayer is called, the doors of the skies are opened, and the du’ a is answered”.

And in another one,

“Seek the response to your du’ as when the armies meet, and

the prayer is called, and when rain falls”.””*

220 AL-Hilali, p. 47.

2 Reported by Muslim (# 757).

222 Reported by Aba Dawid (# 2540), Ibn Majah, and al-Hakim, from Sahl ibn Sa‘d, and authenticated by al-Albani in Sahih al-Jami’ # 3079.

223 Reported by al-Tayalisi in his Musnad (# 2106); authenticated in alSahihah, # 1413.

*4 Reported by al-Shafi't in his al-Umm (1/223); authenticated in al-Sahihah,

4. dua Between the Adhan and Igamah

This is one of the best times for the prayer to be accepted, and what a great blessing it is! Five times every day, while a worshipper is waiting to offer his prayers to Allah, he is given the opportunity to ask from Him his needs of this world and the Hereafter.

Anas ibn Malik narrates that the Prophet (3%) said:

“The dua said between the adhan and the igamah is not re-

fused, therefore offer your du’ as (at this time)”.

5. dua@ During the salah

This time is also one during which du‘ as are answered and accepted by Allah, all Glory and Praise be to Him. Aba Hurayrah narrated that the Prophet (3%) said:

“People will have to stop from looking up at the sky while making du’ a during yalat, or else Allah will snatch their sight

Other narrations forbid looking up at the sky during prayer in general, and this narration shows that the prohibition is even stronger while one is making dua@. This because one has a natural tendency to look upwards while making a du’ a, and, thus, the Prophet (#%) mentioned du ‘a explicitly in this narration.

Some of the postures of the sa/at during which the performance of du’a has been narrated include the following:

1. After the initial zakbir, when one is starting the salah.

2. Before the ruki’ and after one has finished reciting the Quran

— but this is only during the witr or other quniit prayers.

225 Narrated by Ahmad, Aba Dawad (# 521) and al-Tirmidhi (# 212) from Anas ibn Malik. Al-Tirmidhi considered it to be hasan sahih, and Ahmad Shakir agreed with him (1/416). Also see Sahih al-Jami' # 3408.

26Narrated by Muslim, Ahmad, and al-Nasa’i, from Aba Hurayrah, as mentioned in Sahih al-Jami' #5479.

3. After one has stood up from ruka’. Abt Hurayrah narrates the Prophet (#8) used to say, after standing up from rukaz': “Allah is Ever-Hearing to one who praises Him. Our Lord! To You belongs all praise, the weight of the Heavens, and the weight of the earth, and the weight of anything that You desire. O

Allah! cleanse me with ice and water, cleanse me of my sins and mistakes as a white garment is cleansed of dirt!”

4. During the rvka’ itself, for the Prophet (3%) used to say during it: “Glorified be You, O Allah, our Lord, and be Praised! O

5. During the seya#d, and this was the posture during which the

Prophet (#8) made most of his du’ as. 6. While sitting between the two sajdahs.

7. After the final tashahhud, and before the end of the prayer.

These postures are in addition to the du’ as that he (3%) used to do while actually reciting the Qur’an. Hudhayfah narrated in this respect:

“I prayed one night behind the Prophet (3%), and he started reciting Sarah al-Bagarah. I thought to myself, ‘He will surely stop after a hundred verses.’ However, (after he reached a hundred verses), he went on, so I said to myself, ‘He will surely finish the Sarah in this rak‘ ah’. When he finished a/-Bagarah, I thought, ‘He will surely go into ruka’ now’, but he started Sarah al-Nisa, and continued reciting it until he finished. Then he started reciting Sarah Al’ Imran, and he completed its recitation! He was reciting in a very gentle and unhurried manner. Whenever he read a verse in which Allah was glorified, he

227 Narrated by Muslim (476) from ‘Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa. 228 Reported by al-Bukhari and Muslim.

would glorify Allah, and whenever he read a verse which requested something (from Allah), he would request it, and whenever he read a verse that sought refuge in Allah, he would seek refuge in Allah...”

So when a person is reciting the Qur’an in a voluntary prayer, they are encouraged to make dua whenever one occurs in the Quran. This is only for voluntary prayer, as for the obligatory prayers, it is not encouraged.

6. dua while Prostrating

This is the most noble posture that a worshipper can be in, for it

‘is the epitome of humility and submissiveness. And how can it not

be, when a person in prostration lowers his face — the most noble

and sacred part of his body — to the dust, seeking the pleasure of his

Lord? This is why this posture is the most beloved by Allah, all

Glory and Praise be to Him. Abi Hurayrah narrated that the Prophet (8) said:

“The closest any worshipper can be to his Lord is while he is
content_id:
20. Menyebut Dirinya Sendiri Hanya Jika Ia adalah Imam Diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (2) bersabda:

Janganlah seseorang menjadi Imam suatu kaum, lalu hanya menyebut

dirinya sendiri dalam du'a, dan meninggalkan mereka. Jika ia melakukan demikian, maka ia

214 telah mengkhianati mereka.

Meskipun hadith ini mungkin tidak sahih, namun maknanya tetap berlaku, karena Imam bertanggung jawab atas kesejahteraan seluruh jamaah. Oleh karena itu, ketika ia membuat du'a, seperti dalam shalat witir atau qunut, ia tidak boleh berkata, "Ya Allah, ampunilah aku, dan tambahkanlah ilmuku!" melainkan, "Ya Allah! Ampunilah kami, dan tambahkanlah ilmu kami," dan seterusnya. Ini hanya berlaku untuk du'a yang dilakukan dengan suara keras. Adapun du'a yang dilakukan secara diam-diam, seperti dalam sujud atau di akhir shalat, maka Imam diperbolehkan berdoa hanya untuk dirinya sendiri.

214 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi (# 357), Abu Dawud (# 90), dan Ahmad (5/ 250). Al-Tirmidhi mengatakan bahwa hadith ini hasan, namun Ahmad Shakir menunjukkan bahwa mungkin ada kelemahan di dalamnya (2/190). Al-Albani menyatakan bahwa hadith ini lemah

"WAKTU-WAKTU YANG DIANJURKAN UNTUK Du'a

Di antara rahmat Allah yang agung adalah Dia telah mengutamakan waktu-waktu tertentu di atas yang lain. Maka, Dia telah membedakan waktu-waktu ini agar sang hamba dapat dengan penuh harap menantikan kedatangannya, dan dengan demikian berdoa dengan sungguh-sungguh dan ikhlas. Seandainya semua waktu sama, sang hamba tidak akan memiliki semangat dan gairah yang ada selama waktu-waktu yang lebih diberkahi.

Oleh karena itu, penting bagi orang yang menginginkan du'anya dikabulkan untuk memanfaatkan waktu-waktu ini, dan memastikan bahwa du'anya lebih sering dan lebih ikhlas pada saat-saat tersebut.

1. Du'a di Sepertiga Malam Terakhir

Pada waktu ini, ketika kebanyakan makhluk sedang terlelap tidur, sang hamba sejati terjaga, dengan penuh kesungguhan berdoa kepada Penciptanya, merenungi ciptaan Allah, langit dan bumi. Bahkan, Allah menggambarkan orang-orang beriman sejati sebagai mereka yang:

Zeon ret pee @ Opts oh sty) "dan, di waktu-waktu menjelang fajar, mereka memohon ampunan

Tidak hanya itu, Allah sendiri turun kepada hamba-Nya,

dan membuka bagi-Nya pintu-pintu Rahmat dan Ampunan. Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) bersabda:

2 Surah al-Dhariyat, 18.

"Rabb kita turun setiap malam, pada sepertiga malam terakhir, ke langit dunia, dan bertanya: 'Siapakah yang berdu'a kepada-Ku, sehingga Aku dapat mengabulkannya? Siapakah yang meminta kepada-Ku, sehingga Aku dapat memberinya? Siapakah yang meminta ampunan-Ku, sehingga Aku dapat mengampuninya?'"?"*

Dan 'Amr ibn 'Absah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) bersabda:

"Waktu paling dekat seorang hamba dengan Rabbnya adalah pada bagian akhir malam, maka jika kamu dapat menjadi di antara mereka yang berdzikir kepada Allah pada waktu itu, lakukanlah"

Ini adalah motivasi yang besar, kemudian, bagi siapa pun yang benar-benar menginginkan

untuk mendekatkan diri kepada Allah, segala kemuliaan dan pujian bagi-Nya, dan berharap

agar dosa-dosanya diampuni, dan du'anya dikabulkan, untuk bangun

ketika semua orang tertidur, dan berdzikir kepada Allah secara pribadi, sambil terus berdoa kepada-Nya.

Selanjutnya Abu Sa'id dan Abu Hurayrah keduanya meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) bersabda:

"Allah menunggu hingga sepertiga pertama malam berlalu, kemudian Dia turun ke langit dunia dan berkata: 'Adakah yang meminta ampunan? Adakah yang ingin bertaubat? Adakah yang meminta? Adakah yang berdu'a?'

Dan ini terus berlanjut, hingga fajar tiba".

"Rabb kita — segala kemuliaan dan pujian bagi-Nya — turun pada sepertiga malam terakhir ke langit dunia, dan Dia berkata: 'Siapa yang berdu'a, sehingga Aku dapat mengabulkannya? Siapa yang meminta kepada-Ku, sehingga Aku dapat memberinya? Siapa yang memohon ampunan-Ku, sehingga Aku dapat mengampuninya?'"

216 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim, dari Abu Hurayrah, sebagaimana dilaporkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 8021.

Sahih, diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, dan al-Hakim dari 'Amr

ibn 'Absah, dan disahihkan oleh al-Albani dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 1173.

718 Sahih, diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad dan Muslim dari Abu Hurayrah dan Abu Sa'id al-Khudri.

219 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari (#1145), Muslim (# 758) dan lainnya.

Maka itulah mengapa Abu Bakr al-Tartashi berkata, "Dan bab ini ditutup dengan menyatakan: Bukanlah orang yang bijak, dia yang memiliki kebutuhan kepada Allah, namun tidur melewatkan kebutuhannya di malam hari!"

Jadi jika seseorang menginginkan du'anya dikabulkan, inilah waktu terbaik untuk meminta.

2. Suatu Waktu di Malam Hari

Selain hadith tentang du'a pada sepertiga malam terakhir, ada juga beberapa hadith yang memberitahu kita bahwa ada waktu umum di malam hari di mana du'a dikabulkan. Nabi (88) bersabda:

Ada di malam hari suatu waktu, tidaklah seorang Muslim kebetulan meminta

kepada Allah suatu urusan dunia maupun akhirat, kecuali dia akan diberikannya, dan ini terjadi setiap malam."!

3. Du'a Ketika Adzan Dikumandangkan Ini berdasarkan hadith Nabi (#8):

"Dua (du'a) tidak pernah ditolak, atau jarang ditolak: du'a pada saat adzan, dan du'a pada saat bencana,

ketika dua pasukan saling berhadapan".

"Ketika shalat dikumandangkan, pintu-pintu langit dibuka, dan du'a dikabulkan".

Dan dalam riwayat lain,

"Carilah pengabulan du'a kalian ketika pasukan-pasukan bertemu, dan

shalat dikumandangkan, dan ketika hujan turun".""*

220 AL-Hilali, h. 47.

2 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (# 757).

222 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (# 2540), Ibn Majah, dan al-Hakim, dari Sahl ibn Sa'd, dan disahihkan oleh al-Albani dalam Sahih al-Jami' # 3079.

223 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tayalisi dalam Musnad-nya (# 2106); disahihkan dalam al-Sahihah, # 1413.

*4 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Shafi'i dalam al-Umm-nya (1/223); disahihkan dalam al-Sahihah,

4. Du'a Antara Adzan dan Iqamah

Ini adalah salah satu waktu terbaik agar doa dikabulkan, dan alangkah besar berkahnya! Lima kali setiap hari, sementara seorang hamba menunggu untuk mempersembahkan shalatnya kepada Allah, ia diberi kesempatan untuk meminta dari-Nya kebutuhannya di dunia dan akhirat.

Anas ibn Malik meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (3%) bersabda:

"Du'a yang diucapkan antara adzan dan iqamah tidak akan di-

tolak, oleh karena itu panjatkanlah du'a kalian (pada waktu ini)".

5. Du'a Selama Shalat

Waktu ini juga merupakan salah satu waktu di mana du'a dikabulkan dan diterima oleh Allah, segala kemuliaan dan pujian bagi-Nya. Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (3%) bersabda:

"Orang-orang harus berhenti dari memandang ke langit sambil berdu'a selama shalat, atau Allah akan mencabut penglihatan mereka

Riwayat-riwayat lain melarang memandang ke langit selama shalat secara umum, dan riwayat ini menunjukkan bahwa larangannya bahkan lebih kuat saat seseorang sedang berdu'a. Ini karena seseorang memiliki kecenderungan alami untuk memandang ke atas saat berdu'a, dan, dengan demikian, Nabi (#%) menyebutkan du'a secara eksplisit dalam riwayat ini.

Beberapa postur dalam shalat di mana pelaksanaan du'a telah diriwayatkan meliputi berikut ini:

1. Setelah takbir pembuka, ketika seseorang memulai shalat.

2. Sebelum ruku' dan setelah selesai membaca Al-Quran

— tetapi ini hanya berlaku selama shalat witir atau shalat qunut lainnya.

225 Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Abu Dawud (# 521) dan al-Tirmidhi (# 212) dari Anas ibn Malik. Al-Tirmidhi menganggapnya hasan sahih, dan Ahmad Shakir setuju dengannya (1/416). Lihat juga Sahih al-Jami' # 3408.

26Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, Ahmad, dan al-Nasa'i, dari Abu Hurayrah, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Sahih al-Jami' #5479.

3. Setelah seseorang bangkit dari ruku'. Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (#8) biasa mengucapkan, setelah bangkit dari ruku': "Allah Maha Mendengar orang yang memuji-Nya. Ya Rabb kami! Bagi-Mu segala pujian, seberat langit, dan seberat bumi, dan seberat apa pun yang Engkau kehendaki. Ya

Allah! Sucikanlah aku dengan es dan air, sucikanlah aku dari dosa-dosa dan kesalahan-kesalahanku sebagaimana pakaian putih disucikan dari kotoran!"

4. Selama ruku' itu sendiri, karena Nabi (3%) biasa mengucapkan di dalamnya: "Mahasuci Engkau, ya Allah, Rabb kami, dan segala puji bagi-Mu! Ya

5. Selama sujud, dan inilah postur di mana

Nabi (#8) paling banyak melakukan du'a. 6. Saat duduk di antara dua sujud.

7. Setelah tasyahhud akhir, dan sebelum mengakhiri shalat.

Postur-postur ini adalah tambahan dari du'a yang beliau (3%) lakukan saat membaca Al-Qur'an. Hudhayfah meriwayatkan dalam hal ini:

"Suatu malam aku shalat di belakang Nabi (3%), dan beliau mulai membaca Surah al-Baqarah. Aku berkata dalam hati, 'Beliau pasti akan berhenti setelah seratus ayat.' Namun, (setelah mencapai seratus ayat), beliau terus melanjutkan, maka aku berkata pada diri sendiri, 'Beliau pasti akan menyelesaikan surah ini dalam satu raka'at ini'. Ketika beliau menyelesaikan al-Baqarah, aku berpikir, 'Beliau pasti akan ruku' sekarang', tetapi beliau mulai membaca Surah al-Nisa, dan terus membacanya hingga selesai. Kemudian beliau mulai membaca Surah Ali Imran, dan menyelesaikan bacaannya! Beliau membaca dengan cara yang sangat lembut dan tidak terburu-buru. Setiap kali beliau membaca ayat yang di dalamnya Allah diagungkan, beliau

227 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (476) dari 'Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa. 228 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari dan Muslim.

akan bertasbih kepada Allah, dan setiap kali membaca ayat yang memohon sesuatu (kepada Allah), beliau akan memohonnya, dan setiap kali membaca ayat yang meminta perlindungan kepada Allah, beliau akan berlindung kepada Allah..."

Maka ketika seseorang membaca al-Quran dalam salah sunnah, dianjurkan baginya untuk berdoa setiap kali ada du'a yang terdapat dalam al-Quran. Ini hanya berlaku untuk salah sunnah, adapun salah wajib, maka hal itu tidak dianjurkan.

6. Du'a ketika Sujud

Ini adalah posisi paling mulia yang dapat dilakukan seorang hamba, karena ia

merupakan puncak kerendahan hati dan ketundukan. Dan bagaimana tidak,

ketika seseorang dalam keadaan sujud merendahkan wajahnya — bagian tubuh yang paling mulia

dan terhormat — ke tanah, mencari keridhaan

Tuhannya? Inilah mengapa posisi ini adalah yang paling dicintai oleh Allah,

Maha Suci dan Maha Terpuji Dia. Abu Hurayrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda:

"Saat seorang hamba paling dekat dengan Tuhannya adalah ketika ia sedang
content_ar: 20. To Mention Himself Only إذا He is the الإمام وردت الرواية أنه النبي ﷺ (2), said: Let no one be the الإمام of a people, and then only mention himself in the du‘ a, and leave them out. إذا he does لذا, then he 214 has cheated them. وإن كان this الحديث might not be authentic, the meaning of it is applicable, as the الإمام is responsible for the welfare of the entire congregation. لذلك, عندما he makes a dw’ 4, مثل in the witr or qunut الصلاة, he should not say, “O الله, forgive me, and increase my العلم!” but rather, “O الله! Forgive us, and increase our العلم,” and لذا forth. This only applies to the du‘a that is done out loud. أما the الدعاء that one does silently, مثل in sujad (prostration) or at the end of the الصلاة, then the الإمام is allowed to pray only for himself. 214 Reported by al-الترمذي (# 357), Aba Dawad (# 90), and أحمد (5/ 250). Al-الترمذي said that it is حسن, but أحمد Shakir pointed out that it might have some الضعف in it (2/190). Al-Albani stated that it is weak "THE RECOMMENDED TIMES FOR Du'4 Of the great الرحمة of الله is that He has favoured certain times over others. وهكذا, He has distinguished these timings لذا that the worshipper can eagerly anticipate their arrival, and وهكذا pray earnestly and sincerely. Had all timings been the same, the worshipper would not have had the enthusiasm and fervor that exists خلال more blessed times. لذلك, من المهم that the one who desires that his الدعاء be answered utilise these times, and ensure that his الدعاء be more frequent and sincere خلال them. 1. Du‘a@ in the Last ثالثاً of the Night خلال this time, عندما most of creation is in a deep sleep, the true worshipper is awake, earnestly praying to his Creator, reflecting upon الله’s creation, the heavens and earth. In fact, الله describes the true المؤمنون as those who: Zeon ret pee @ Opts oh sty) “and, in the hours of dawn, they seek المغفرة from their Not only that, but الله Himself comes down to His servant, and opens for Him the doors of الرحمة and المغفرة. Aba Hurayrah narrated that النبي ﷺ (#8) said: 2 seerah al-Dhariyat, 18. “Our Lord descends every night, خلال the last ثالثاً of it, to the skies of this الدنيا, and asks: ‘Who is making du’ a to Me, لذا that I can respond to him? Who is asking Me, لذا that I can give him? Who is asking for My المغفرة, لذا that I can forgive him?’”?"* And ‘Amr ibn ‘Absah reported that النبي ﷺ (#8) said: “The closest any worshipper can be to his Lord is خلال the last part of the night, لذا إذا you can be amongst those who remember الله at that time, then do لذا” This is a huge incentive, then, for any person who truly desires to come closer to الله, all Glory and Praise be to Him, and wishes .to have his الذنوب forgiven, and his الدعاء@ responded to, to wake up عندما everyone else is asleep, and remember الله privately, and all the بينما praying to Him. علاوة على ذلك Aba Sa‘id and Aba Hurayrah both reported that النبي ﷺ (#8) said: “الله waits حتى the أولاً ثالثاً of the night has passed, then He descends down to the skies of this الدنيا and says: ‘Is there any that seeks المغفرة? Is there any who wishes to repent? Is there any who is asking? Is there any who is making a du‘ a?’ And this continues, حتى the break of dawn”. “Our Lord — all Glory and Praise be to Him — comes down in the last ثالثاً of the night to the skies of this الدنيا, and He says: ‘Who is there that is making a dw’ @, لذا that I can respond to him? Whois there that is asking Me, لذا that I can give him? Who is there that is seeking my المغفرة, لذا that I can forgive him?” 216 Reported by البخاري and مسلم, from Aba Hurayrah, as reported in صحيح al-Jami' # 8021. Authentic, narrated by al-الترمذي, al-Nasa’i, and al-Hakim from ‘Amr ibn ‘Absah, and authenticated by al-Albani in صحيح al-Jami’ # 1173. 718 Authentic, narrated by أحمد and مسلم from Aba Hurayrah and Aba Sa‘id al-Khudri. 219 Reported by البخاري (#1145), مسلم (# 758) and others. ومن ثَم why Abi Bakr al-Tartashi said, “And this الباب is closed by stating: He is not a wise man who has a need to الله, yet he sleeps away his need at night!” لذا إذا a person wishes that his du'@ be answered, this is the best time to ask. 2, An Hour of the Night بالإضافة إلى the الحديث pertaining to du‘a at the last ثالثاً of the night, there are أيضاً الأحاديث informing us that there is a general time at night خلال which du'as are responded to. النبي ﷺ (88) said: There is at night an hour, ne مسلم happens to be asking الله any matter of this الدنيا or الآخرة, except that he will be given it, and this (occurs) every night.”! 3. الدعاء عندما the Adhan is Called This is based on the الحديث of النبي ﷺ (#8): “Two (du'as) are never rejected, or rarely rejected: the du‘a خلال the call for الصلاة, and the du‘a خلال the calamity, عندما the two armies attack بعضهم بعضاً”. “عندما the الصلاة is called, the doors of the skies are opened, and the du’ a is answered”. And in another one, “Seek the response to your du’ as عندما the armies meet, and the الصلاة is called, and عندما rain falls”.””* 220 AL-Hilali, p. 47. 2 Reported by مسلم (# 757). 222 Reported by Aba Dawid (# 2540), ابن ماجه, and al-Hakim, from Sahl ibn Sa‘d, and authenticated by al-Albani in صحيح al-Jami’ # 3079. 223 Reported by al-Tayalisi in his Musnad (# 2106); authenticated in alSahihah, # 1413. *4 Reported by al-Shafi't in his al-Umm (1/223); authenticated in al-Sahihah, 4. الدعاء Between the Adhan and Igamah This is one of the best times for the الصلاة to be accepted, and what a great البركة it is! Five times every day, بينما a worshipper is waiting to offer his الصلوات to الله, he is given the opportunity to ask from Him his needs of this الدنيا and الآخرة. أنس ibn مالك narrates that النبي ﷺ (3%) said: “The الدعاء said between the adhan and the igamah is not re- fused, لذلك offer your du’ as (at this time)”. 5. الدعاء@ خلال the الصلاة This time is أيضاً one خلال which du‘ as are answered and accepted by الله, all Glory and Praise be to Him. Aba Hurayrah narrated that النبي ﷺ (3%) said: “People will have to stop from looking up at the sky بينما making du’ a خلال yalat, or else الله will snatch their sight Other narrations forbid looking up at the sky خلال الصلاة in general, and this narration shows that the prohibition is even stronger بينما one is making الدعاء@. This لأن one has a natural tendency to look upwards بينما making a du’ a, and, وهكذا, النبي ﷺ (#%) mentioned du ‘a explicitly in this narration. Some of the postures of the sa/at خلال which the performance of du’a has been narrated include the following: 1. بعد the initial zakbir, عندما one is starting the الصلاة. 2. قبل the ruki’ and بعد one has finished reciting the القرآن الكريم — but this is only خلال the witr or other quniit الصلوات. 225 Narrated by أحمد, Aba Dawad (# 521) and al-الترمذي (# 212) from أنس ibn مالك. Al-الترمذي considered it to be حسن صحيح, and أحمد Shakir agreed with him (1/416). أيضاً see صحيح al-Jami' # 3408. 26Narrated by مسلم, أحمد, and al-Nasa’i, from Aba Hurayrah, as mentioned in صحيح al-Jami' #5479. 3. بعد one has stood up from ruka’. Abt Hurayrah narrates النبي ﷺ (#8) used to say, بعد standing up from rukaz': “الله is Ever-Hearing to one who praises Him. Our Lord! To You belongs all praise, the weight of the Heavens, and the weight of the earth, and the weight of anything that You desire. O الله! cleanse me with ice and water, cleanse me of my الذنوب and mistakes as a white garment is cleansed of dirt!” 4. خلال the rvka’ itself, for النبي ﷺ (3%) used to say خلال it: “Glorified be You, O الله, our Lord, and be Praised! O 5. خلال the seya#d, and this was the posture خلال which النبي ﷺ (#8) made most of his du’ as. 6. بينما sitting between the two sajdahs. 7. بعد the final التشهد, and قبل the end of the الصلاة. These postures are بالإضافة إلى the du’ as that he (3%) used to do بينما actually reciting the Qur’an. Hudhayfah narrated in this الاحترام: “I prayed one night behind النبي ﷺ (3%), and he started reciting Sarah al-Bagarah. I thought to myself, ‘He will surely stop بعد a hundred الآيات.’ غير أن, (بعد he reached a hundred الآيات), he went on, لذا I said to myself, ‘He will surely finish the Sarah in this rak‘ ah’. عندما he finished a/-Bagarah, I thought, ‘He will surely go into ruka’ now’, but he started Sarah al-Nisa, and continued reciting it حتى he finished. Then he started reciting Sarah Al’ Imran, and he completed its التلاوة! He was reciting in a very gentle and unhurried manner. كلما he read a الآية in which الله was glorified, he 227 Narrated by مسلم (476) from ‘Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa. 228 Reported by البخاري and مسلم. would glorify الله, and كلما he read a الآية which requested something (from الله), he would request it, and كلما he read a الآية that sought refuge in الله, he would seek refuge in الله...” لذا عندما a person is reciting the Qur’an in a voluntary الصلاة, they are encouraged to make الدعاء كلما one occurs in the القرآن الكريم. This is only for voluntary الصلاة, أما the obligatory الصلوات, it is not encouraged. 6. الدعاء بينما Prostrating This is the most noble posture that a worshipper can be in, for it ‘is the epitome of التواضع and submissiveness. And how can it not be, عندما a person in prostration lowers his face — the most noble and sacred part of his الجسد — to the dust, seeking the pleasure of his Lord? This is why this posture is the most beloved by الله, all Glory and Praise be to Him. Abi Hurayrah narrated that النبي ﷺ (8) said: “The closest any worshipper can be to his Lord is بينما he is
